data.title: NB Bancorp agrees to buy Provident Bancorp for $212M in stock, cash deal
data.description:
NB Bancorp (NASDAQ:NBBK) has agreed to acquire Provident Bancorp (NASDAQ:PVBC) in a stock and cash transaction that's expected to close in Q4 2025, the lenders said Thursday.

Based on NB's (NASDAQ:NBBK) stock price of $16.62 at the close of business on June 4, the deal is estimated to be valued at $211.8M.

Under the terms of the merger agreement, which was unanimously approved by both company boards, PVBC stockholders will receive for each share of PVBC common stock, at the holder's election, either 0.691 shares of NBBK common stock or $13.00 in cash, subject to allocation procedures to ensure that 50% of the shares of PVBC shares will receive the stock consideration.

NBBK is planning to issue about 5.9M of its common shares in conjunction with the merger, diluting the company's tangible book value by ~6.1%. The deal is expected to have an earn-back period of ~2.7 years.

As part of the merger, Joseph B. Reilly, president and CEO of Provident (NASDAQ:PVBC), will join the board of Needham Bank (NBBK).

The combined company will operate 18 branches across Metrowest, Greater Boston, the North Shore in Massachusetts and Southern New Hampshire. Total assets at transaction close are expected to be ~$7.1B, with $5.9Bin total deposits and $6.1B in total loans.

"This merger allows Needham Bank to expand into attractive market areas on the Massachusetts North Shore and in Southern New Hampshire where we already have a concentration of business clients," said Needham Bank Chairman, President and CEO Joseph P. Campanelli.

PVBC shares gained 4% in after-hours trading, while NBBK was little changed.

data.title: AirT announces $100 million multi-year financing agreement with institutional investors
data.description:
• Air T (NASDAQ:AIRT) announced on Thursday a key financing agreement, involving its wholly owned subsidiary - AAM 24-1, with its existing institutional investors, growing the transaction from $30 million to $100 million, extending repayment timelines and increasing financial flexibility.
  • As part of the restructuring, Air T and its subsidiary will receive committed, non-recourse capital in scheduled disbursements through 2027, with the full note maturing in 2035.
  • The capital will also be allocated to support the growth and strategic initiatives of Crestone Air Partners, a full-service aviation asset management platform, and other Air T subsidiaries.
  • "This modified financing structure significantly enhances our capital flexibility and allows us to pursue long-term value creation with greater certainty," said Nick Swenson, Chairman and CEO of Air T.

data.title: Peloton CEO rejects talk of a sale, vows to restore growth
data.description:
• Peloton (NASDAQ:PTON) CEO Peter Stern dismissed the idea of a sale, pledging instead to revive the business and reignite demand for its fitness hardware.
  • “I was not hired to sell this company,” Stern said in a conversation at Bloomberg’s Tech Summit Thursday. “I was hired to bring this company back to growth, reinvigorate it, and that is happening.”
  • While subscription sales continue to outpace equipment purchases, Stern is not interested in scaling up memberships at the expense of the equipment business.
  • He hopes to revitalize equipment sales by expanding Peloton’s (NASDAQ:PTON) presence in hotel gyms and augmenting the use of artificial intelligence to create a personalized coaching experience for users.
  • Although Wall Street analysts view the stock more favorably, investors are still waiting for the company to sufficiently turn a corner. 
  • Last quarter, Peloton (NASDAQ:PTON) reported a 13% drop in sales with connected fitness revenue down 27% and subscription revenue down 4%. And although profitability improved by 73%, the improvement came at the expense of deep cost cutting, specifically in ad spending and research & development.

data.title: IAC announces pricing of senior secured notes offering
data.description:
• IAC (NASDAQ:IAC) announced on Thursday that its wholly owned subsidiary, Dotdash Meredith (DDM), has agreed to sell $400 million aggregate principal amount of 7.625% senior secured notes due 2032 in a private offering. 
  • The notes will bear interest at an interest rate of 7.625% per annum and will be issued at 100.00% of their face value.  DDM's obligations under the Notes will be guaranteed on a senior secured basis by certain of DDM's subsidiaries.
  • DDM intends to use the proceeds of the offering, together with the borrowings under DDM's new $700 million term loan B facility and cash on hand, to repay all the indebtedness outstanding under DDM's existing $1.18 billion term loan B-1 facility and to pay related fees and expenses. 
  • The closings of each of the Offering and new term loan B facility are expected to occur on or about June 16, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Broadcom sees AI semiconductor revenue rising to $5.1B in Q3
Capital One board gives CEO stock grant valued at $30M for Discover integration
data.description:
Capital One Financial's (NYSE:COF) compensation committee and the board's independent directors approved one-time stock awards to Chairman and CEO Richard D. Fairbank and other named executive officers for their work in integrating Discover Financial's business into Capital One, the company said Thursday.

Fairbank was granted 153,351 restricted stock units, with a grant date value of ~$30M. His award will fully vest on June 3, 2030, and will settle half in cash, based on the company's average stock price over the 15 trading days preceding the vesting date, and half in stock.

The other executives granted RSUs are: Andrew M. Young 15,336 RSUs ($3.0M), Frank G. LaPrade, III: 12,780 RSUs ($2.5M), Matthew W. Cooper: 28,115 RSUs ($5.5M), and Sanjiv Yajnik: 12,780 RSUs ($2.5M).

The RSUs granted to the named executive officers will vest in full on June 3, 2028, and will settle 100% in stock, the company said.

Capital One (NYSE:COF) closed on its acquisition of Discover  on May 19, 2025.

data.title: Perplexity reports extreme growth as CEO says users 'are tired of legacy browsers': report
data.description:
Perplexity's artificial intelligence-powered search engine is recording 20% month-over-month growth, with queries reaching 780M in May, said its CEO Aravind Srinivas on Thursday, according to a report by TechCrunch.

Srinivas, speaking during the Bloomberg Tech Summit, said his search engine will reach 1B queries per week if it can sustain its current growth rate, the report said. He also said internet surfers are tired of using the same old browsers, such as Google's (NASDAQ:GOOGL)(NASDAQ:GOOG) Chrome.

"If people are in the browser, it's infinite retention," Srinivas said, according to the report. "Everything in the search bar, everything on the new tab page, everything you're doing on the sidecar, any of the pages you're in, these are all going to be extra queries per active user, as well as seeking new users who just are tired of legacy browsers, like Chrome."  

Perplexity is gearing up to release Comet, which is a type of agentic search engine that can complete user tasks. Those interested can join a waitlist here.

"Comet will have a native virtual meets recording, transcription and searches over them," Srinivas posted last month on X. "Won't be part of the first release, but very fast follow up. As for release date: it's going to take a min of three weeks and a max of five weeks. Reliability and latency have improved over the last few weeks. The last 10-20% is so much harder than we thought. Everyone's working super hard to get the browser out in your hands. Bear with us until then."

Perplexity, which is backed by major investors such as Nvidia (NVDA) and Amazon (AMZN) co-founder Jeff Bezos, is reportedly in the late stages of a new funding round that would value the startup at $14B. It was valued at $9B in December.

The search engine added a new feature last month that allows users to buy products directly in the chat, utilizing PayPal (PYPL) or Venmo, for the checkout process.

Despite the rising popularity of new search engines such as Perplexity, Google's search engine continues to hold about 90% of the global market share, according to various reports. Microsoft's (MSFT) Bing ranks second with about 4%.
